title: digitalis purpurea f. parviflora
intro: Digitalis purpurea f. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered foxglove, is a variant of the common foxglove plant, characterized by its smaller flowers. This plant contains the same medicinal compounds as its parent species, including digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various heart conditions and arrhythmias. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea f. parviflora have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, strengthening the heart, and improving circulation. The herbal preparation of this plant is typically made into a tincture, tea, or extract, and is often used in homeopathic and traditional medicine practices.
